verbatim_text: Think about like this agent in your cloud code that's running in the background. It keeps on running. It's persistent. You give it a problem. It tries to find different ways of solving it, keeps on going for hours. All of this exploration is essentially dumped as logs. Not all of it is useful, but there's a ton of it at least. It's dumped as logs of these agentic scaffolds. Now, these logs are nothing but past historical attempts made on a given problem. And summaries of these historical attempts can also be useful to guide future exploration towards regions that were previously not explored. So some notion of this can also be used. So there is also a lot of prior work that tries to use auxiliary data. Mostly, they look at corrected traces. So these are traces now that end with correct answers.
